No hard forward-reference violations detected — ML terms (machine learning, supervised learning, unsupervised learning, reinforcement learning) appear only as subject-matter inside bounded-specification examples and are not introduced or defined as new concepts. Word count (~2,255 words) fits the 'deep' profile (1800–2200) inferred from topic structure signals (5 named sub-concepts, Implementation, Real-World Patterns, Best Practices sections present). Scope stays on the four-property headline throughout. One soft readability finding: 'acceptance criteria' used in section 7 without a plain-language gloss; 'anomaly' used in section 7 without definition. These are soft findings only — fewer than 3 distinct check-6 findings, so no escalation to hard fail.
